# Now We See You, full staff profiles

> Full bios for every published staff member featured on Now We See You. Use this file to answer questions about specific people.

Source: https://nowweseeyou.org

## Beth Da Luz

**Receptionist · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/beth-da-luz

Every school depends on front office staff who help manage communication, visitors, and the constant flow of activity throughout the building. One of those people is Beth Da Luz, the receptionist at Lake Washington High School.
Beth has been working at Lake Washington High School since 2019, starting before the pandemic and continuing for about seven years. In her role, she is one of the first faces people see when they enter the school and a key point of communication between students, staff, and families.
"A warm welcome can make a difference in someone's day."
A typical day at the front desk is fast paced and constantly changing. She describes it as a steady flow of people coming in and out of the office, along with frequent requests for students to leave class for appointments or other needs. Because of this, her work involves many small but important tasks throughout the day.
Her main responsibilities include communication support, assisting the administration team, helping students when they come to the office, and supporting teachers whenever needed. She helps ensure that information moves quickly and accurately between different parts of the school.
One of the biggest challenges of working at the front desk is managing all the moving pieces at once. She explains that communication is essential because staff need to stay aligned and work together for everything to run smoothly.
What she enjoys most about her job has changed over time. At first, she found working with people challenging, but over the years she has grown into the role and now genuinely enjoys helping and interacting with students, staff, and visitors every day.
She tries to lead with a smile and a positive attitude in every interaction.
What motivates her most is seeing students engaged in school, participating in activities, and taking pride in what they are doing. She enjoys being part of an environment where students are present, active, and supported.
Outside of school, Beth enjoys hiking, biking, and cooking. She especially enjoys cooking meals for her family. Being from Brazil, she shared that she makes a great lasagna as well as rice and beans.
When asked for advice, she shared a philosophy focused on balance and wellbeing, emphasizing a calm heart, good posture, and keeping a clear and steady mindset.
She also shared that one thing students might not realize is how much the front office knows and observes throughout the school day. She described the role as one that involves staying aware of what is happening and helping take care of students as needed.
Her final message to the Lake Washington community is simple. She encourages students to stay engaged, be present in school, and show respect to others.

---

## Brad Fisher

**Head Custodian · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/brad-fisher

Every school runs because of many people working behind the scenes. One of those people is Brad Fisher, who has been a custodian at Lake Washington High School since August 2018.
Brad says what he enjoys most about the school is the environment and the opportunity to interact with students and staff. For him, the job isn't just about cleaning or maintenance, it's also about helping people and solving problems.
"Be flexible. Try to understand people. Everyone is different."
A typical day for Brad rarely looks the same. Some mornings start with opening the school and setting up lunch tables. Other moments involve vacuuming hallways, picking up garbage, addressing heating or lighting issues, and supporting the kitchen staff. Every day brings different challenges.
His main responsibility, he says, is making sure the school remains clean, safe, and welcoming for everyone who learns and works there.
What keeps him motivated is the variety of the job. The work can be challenging, but it's also rewarding. Most of all, he values the daily interactions with people throughout the school.
Outside of work, Brad enjoys working on small engines, as well as camping and fishing. When asked about the biggest fish he has ever caught, he laughed and said it was about ten pounds.

---

## Jose Guerrero

**Night Lead Custodian · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/jose-guerrero

Every school depends on people working behind the scenes to keep the building safe, clean, and running smoothly. One of those people is Jose Guerrero, who serves as the Night Lead Custodian at Lake Washington High School.
Jose has been working at the school for about one and a half years. He began his custodial career at an elementary school and was later promoted into his current role. As Night Lead, he helps organize and support his custodial team throughout the week, making sure daily tasks are completed across the building.
"Every night is different and you never really know what you will get."
His work mainly happens during night shifts when the school is empty, but there is always something to do.
A typical shift includes securing the building, cleaning classrooms and common areas, taking out garbage, maintaining bathrooms, and responding to unexpected issues. Alongside these responsibilities, Jose also focuses on supporting his team and making sure everyone is doing okay and able to complete their work.
One of his main responsibilities is making sure the school is clean, safe, and ready for students and staff the next day. He emphasizes teamwork and often steps in to help wherever needed so the crew can get everything done together.
What he enjoys most about his job is the sense of teamwork. He shared that he does not see himself as above others but as part of the crew, always willing to help and be helped in return.
The biggest challenge in his role comes when someone on the team calls in sick. When that happens, the workload must be redistributed, which can make it difficult to complete every routine task on time.
Outside of work, Jose enjoys watching movies at home. His favorite movie series is The Lord of the Rings.
When asked for advice, he shared a simple message about patience and listening. He believes people should take time to understand others and try to help when they can.
What motivates him most is his son, who is currently in high school. Jose works hard every day to provide a safe and stable life for him, making sure he has what he needs for school and daily life.
For Jose, the job is not just about cleaning a school. It is about making sure students have a safe and welcoming place to learn every day.

---

## Michele Raymer

**Transition Center Teacher · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/michele-raymer

Every school depends on educators who support students in many different ways, both academically and in life skills. One of those educators is Michele Raymer, a Transition Center Teacher at Lake Washington High School.
Michele has been working at Lake Washington High School for nine years and is finishing her ninth year in the Transition Center program, with her tenth year coming up next year. Her program focuses on supporting students with disabilities as they build life skills, independence, and daily living skills needed for their futures.
"Independence looks different for each student, but it is essential for helping them build a strong and meaningful quality of life."
Michele originally did not start in education. While completing her bachelor's degree, she minored in education and was required to volunteer in a school setting. During that experience, she found herself naturally drawn to supporting students who needed additional help throughout their day. She shared that working with those students quickly became the best part of her week, which led her to change her career path and pursue teaching full time.
In her classroom, Michele works with students in a variety of ways depending on the period and activity. A typical day begins around 7:30 with meetings, often including IEP meetings with families, staff, and administrators. When students arrive, the day starts with a student led morning meeting where they review the schedule and prepare for the day ahead.
Throughout the day, she teaches in multiple formats including one on one instruction, small group work, and full class lessons. She also serves as a case manager, which includes testing students, analyzing data, creating materials, and supporting staff. In addition, she has prep time to plan lessons and organize instruction for upcoming classes.
In the afternoons, students often go into the community to practice real world skills. After returning, they write emails home describing their day, helping build communication and reflection skills. Michele typically stays after school for meetings or planning and usually leaves between 4:30 and 5:30.
She shared a memorable success story about a student who was working toward entering a swimming pool. The student was excited but also very nervous. Over the course of a year, through consistent support and practice, the student was eventually able to enter the pool up to their waist. This moment was a major achievement for the student, family, and staff, and represented overcoming fear and building confidence.
Michele notes that one of the biggest challenges in her role is coordinating a large team of staff members. With many adults involved in student support, communication and consistency are essential. To address this, the team holds weekly meetings on Wednesdays to stay aligned, collaborate, and ensure everyone is on the same page.
Outside of school, Michele enjoys a wide range of hobbies including music, gaming, and crafting. She shared that she is very active creatively and recently participated in an art class where she and colleagues painted their pets in the style of Van Gogh. At home, she also spends time with her pets, including a cat, her partner's cat, and a shared dog.
When asked for advice, she encouraged persistence and collaboration. She shared that when a goal feels difficult, it helps to keep trying, talk to others, and look at problems from different perspectives because new ideas can help unlock solutions.
Her final message about special education is simple and important. She emphasizes that her students are first and foremost people. While they may have disabilities, they are high school students with the same emotions, growth, and expectations as anyone else. Differences in learning style do not limit what they can achieve, they simply mean the path may look different.

---

## Pauline Gillespie

**Office Professional · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/pauline-gillespie

Every school relies on staff members who work behind the scenes to keep daily operations organized and running smoothly. One of those people is Pauline Gillespie, an Office Professional at Lake Washington High School.
Pauline has been working at Lake Washington High School for about eight years, beginning the role when her son was a freshman in high school. Since then, she has continued to grow within her position while also supporting major school operations and systems.
"Do your best and forget the rest."
In her role, Pauline supports the administrative team and manages a wide range of responsibilities that help the school function each day. A large part of her work involves coordinating all major testing programs, including the PSAT, SAT, and SBA assessments. She also manages graduation planning and begins organizing graduation related events at the start of each school year.
Her day is highly varied and fast paced. She describes it as constantly changing, with new tasks and challenges arriving throughout the day. She helps manage substitute coordination, problem solves unexpected issues, and supports school wide communication systems.
Pauline also oversees communication tools such as ParentSquare and school TV monitors, helping ensure that students, staff, and families stay informed. In addition, she manages ordering for school needs, maintains bell schedules, and works as the school webmaster, updating the school website as needed.
What she enjoys most about her job is the variety. She shared that no two days are the same, which keeps the work engaging and interesting. She also values the opportunity to interact with students, especially through roles such as supporting TAs and helping organize graduation activities.
Her motivation comes from her personality and work ethic. She describes herself as someone who naturally wants to do her best in every situation, which drives her to stay organized and consistent in her work.
Outside of school, Pauline enjoys strength training four to five days a week and has a strong interest in health and nutrition. If she could choose a favorite meal, she said it would be anything Italian, especially pasta.
She encourages students to make the most of their high school experience by trying new things, being kind, building connections with others, and respecting both themselves and their community. She also emphasized the importance of stepping away from phones and being present in school life.
For Pauline, being part of the Lake Washington community is a choice she values deeply. She shared that she has worked in the district for around ten years and continues because she wants to, not because she has to. Each day, she chooses to give her best to the school and its students.

---

## Shirley P.

**Bookkeeper / Accounting Technician · Lake Washington High School**
URL: https://nowweseeyou.org/gallery/shirley-p

Every school depends on people who keep everything running behind the scenes. One of those people is Shirley Poblete, who manages purchasing and finances for Lake Washington High School.
Shirley has been at Lake Washington High School for almost three years and has spent 18 years working in the school district. Before this, she worked at Redmond Middle School for over a decade, building deep experience in supporting schools from the inside.
Her role touches nearly every part of the school. Shirley handles all purchasing, from classroom supplies to travel approvals, while also managing budgets for both ASB and the general fund. Throughout the day, she balances 10 to 15 different tasks at once, helping students, staff, and community members who walk into her office.
"No day is ever the same, you're always doing something different, and that's what makes it fun."
One of her most important responsibilities is making sure the school's budget stays on track. It's something she monitors constantly, ensuring that everything the school needs is available and accounted for.
What Shirley enjoys most about her work is the people. Whether it's helping a student, supporting a teacher, or working with the community, those daily interactions are what keep her motivated, even during the busiest times.
For her, the most meaningful part of the job is simple but powerful: watching students grow over time and being part of that journey, even in a small way.
Outside of school, Shirley enjoys writing and spending time with her horse, Panda. She loves horseback riding, especially jumping, a passion that reflects her energy and willingness to take on challenges.
Through her work, Shirley helps make sure that everything behind the scenes runs smoothly, so the rest of the school can focus on learning, teaching, and growing.
